text: Peace Studies: An Introduction Chapter 7: Constructing A Peace Education School As we move into the practical application of peace studies and education it is essential that we examine the processes, obstacles and ethical issues that must be addressed if a school is to truly be a peace-education institution. The reform of education is fundamentally a social justice issue and the creation of curricula that brings students beyond the level of conflict resolution and into the creation of conditions that abolish all forms of inequality are some of the central issues that will be addressed. A critical survey of major educational philosophies, past obstacles to peace education and methods of curriculum change and teaching practices that empower the learner as a thinking member of a global community will be examined in some detail. The goal of this book is to challenge education, and those who practice it and facilitate it, and those who fund it, to take peace-education seriously as an integral part of both a free and an equal democracy.
